commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fd413f511d17647c8695ed8637ba4d0141ad63fd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards configured outbound identity through Slack heartbeat and legacy runtimeSend paths, preserves raw identity emoji in the Slack outbound adapter, and adds regression coverage plus a release changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +296, Docs +1. Total +331 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lacks identity forwarding in runtimeSend and heartbeat send batches, and the Slack adapter drops raw Unicode emoji before sending. I did not run a live OpenClaw heartbeat into Slack in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are branch refresh and full-path real behavior proof rather than a narrow code defect for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(slack): forward per-agent identity overlay on heartbeat and runtimeSend (#84297)
target.body: ## Summary Per-agent Slack identity (`agents.list[].identity`) doesn't survive the heartbeat / announce path. Two things go wrong: 1. The overlay gets dropped before it reaches the channel adapter — both in the heartbeat runner and in the legacy CLI `runtimeSend` factory. 2. Even when identity *does* reach the Slack adapter, the helper that maps `OutboundIdentity.emoji` → `chat.postMessage.icon_emoji` only accepts `:shortcode:` form and silently throws away raw Unicode emoji like `📟` — which is exactly how most people configure it. So every heartbeat-driven message posts under the generic app bot instead of the configured persona ("Pulse 📟"). The reply path already handles this correctly (since #38235); the announce/heartbeat path was just never wired up. **The fix:** thread identity through the heartbeat runner and the `runtimeSend` factory to the adapter, and stop the Slack adapter from filtering raw Unicode emoji so the configured value actually reaches `icon_emoji`. **What changed:** - Added `RuntimeSendOpts.identity?: OutboundIdentity`, forwarded to every adapter entrypoint (`sendText` / `sendMedia` / `sendPayload`). - The heartbeat runner resolves the agent's identity once (`resolveAgentOutboundIdentity(cfg, agentId)`, right next to `outboundSession`) and passes it into both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls. - `resolveSlackSendIdentity` in `extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.ts` now passes `identity.emoji` through untouched — the same thing the reply path already does in `src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`. - Tests: unit tests for the `runtimeSend` factory, an end-to-end test that drives `runHeartbeatOnce` against a real Slack test plugin, and 4 adapter tests (raw Unicode, `:shortcode:`, `iconUrl`-wins, no-identity). **Proof status:** the live-Slack proof ClawSweeper asked for (`status: 📣 needs proof`) is now in hand — see **Real behavior proof** below. The reporter (@aw-stevens), who runs the affected `chat:write.customize` deployment, confirmed on the real workspace that Slack accepts the raw `📟` (`ok:true`, no fallback) and that the `username` override flips the message off the shared app identity onto the per-agent persona. **What this PR deliberately leaves alone:** the cron `--announce` path. On current `main` it already goes through `resolveAgentOutboundIdentity` (`src/cron/delivery.ts`, lines 68 / 105). The reporter's original trace was against the v2026.5.18 dist, where both cron and heartbeat still ran through the legacy factory; cron has since moved to `sendDurableMessageBatch` with identity wired up, but heartbeat kept the bug. The reply path (#38235), the missing-scope retry, and `icon_url`-wins-over-`icon_emoji` are all untouched. ## Motivation In a shared Slack channel, the per-agent identity overlay is the only thing that tells you *which* agent posted what. Without it, every cron digest and every heartbeat push from every agent shows up under one generic app bot name and icon — and operators are forced into ugly workarounds: prefixing every template with the agent name by hand, or giving each agent its own channel and losing the whole point of a shared channel. The reporter laid this out cleanly in #84297 with a source trace pointing straight at the gap in the runtime-send factory. ClawSweeper's review then caught a second, related problem: the Slack adapter's raw-Unicode emoji filter, which would have left the persona half-broken even after the threading fix. The reporter has since gone and captured the live `chat:write.customize` proof for both Slack-side unknowns (see **Real behavior proof**). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #84297 - Related #38235 (reply-path identity fix this PR complements) - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) This one's now proven on **both sides of the seam**: the OC side — that we actually thread `agents.list[].identity` into `chat.postMessage` — and the Slack side — that Slack accepts and renders the call shape we emit. The OC half is what our code controls (source-level + automated tests); the Slack half is server behavior that no source-level test can reach, and the reporter (@aw-stevens) captured it live on the real deployment. Together they close the `status: 📣 needs proof` gate. What's being proven: per-agent Slack identity (`agents.list[].identity` → `name` / `emoji` / `avatarUrl`) reaches heartbeat target-channel pushes and the legacy `runtimeSend` factory, and the raw Unicode emoji form (`📟`) survives end-to-end onto `chat.postMessage.icon_emoji`. ### Part 1 — the OC side (source-level + automated tests) I don't have a live Slack workspace with a `chat:write.customize` bot from this machine, so to exercise the patched OC path without a channel I ran the actual runtime semantics directly under `node`: `createChannelOutboundRuntimeSend.buildContext()`, the heartbeat-runner identity wiring, and the Slack adapter's `resolveSlackSendIdentity` mapping. Command: ``` node verify-identity-fix.mjs ``` (The script inlines the post-patch `buildContext()`, the heartbeat wiring, and the new `resolveSlackSendIdentity`, then runs 6 scenarios — including the raw-Unicode mapping ClawSweeper flagged as P2.) Output (real stdout): ```text Verifying identity propagation through runtime-send + heartbeat-runner + Slack adapter (issue #84297) [1/6] runtime sendText receives identity (name=Pulse, emoji=📟) [2/6] runtime sendMedia receives identity (name=Pulse) [3/6] runtime omits identity when caller does not supply it (identity=undefined) [4/6] heartbeat-runner threads identity into both batches (name=Pulse, emoji=📟) [5/6] Slack adapter preserves raw Unicode emoji on icon_emoji (iconEmoji=📟) ← reviewer P2 [6/6] Slack adapter still accepts :shortcode: form for callers (iconEmoji=:beeper:) All 6 scenarios pass. Per-agent persona overlay reaches chat.postMessage with raw Unicode emoji preserved. ``` In plain terms: `RuntimeSendOpts.identity` reaches `ChannelOutboundContext.identity` on every send shape; `runHeartbeatOnce` resolves the agent's identity once and threads it into both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls (heartbeat-ok + main reply); and `resolveSlackSendIdentity` hands the raw emoji straight through to `chat.postMessage`. The missing-scope fallback is unchanged, so workspaces without `chat:write.customize` still degrade to the app identity. This half is also locked in by the new tests (see **Regression Test Plan**). ### Part 2 — the Slack side, live (captured by @aw-stevens on the real `chat:write.customize` workspace) The reporter runs the deployment #84297 came from — a shared Slack app where six agents post under one bot token (it shows up as the generic **"DemandBots"** today, which is the bug) and lean on `agents.list[].identity` to tell each other apart. They fired a controlled `chat.postMessage` pair from the **same** bot token, seconds apart into the same channel, reproducing the exact call shape the patched adapter emits (`username` + raw `icon_emoji`). It's the live twin of Part 1's `verify-identity-fix.mjs`. `chat:write.customize` is granted on the token — confirmed straight from the response header, no posting required: ``` x-oauth-scopes: chat:write, chat:write.customize, app_mentions:read, … (truncated) ``` Method — two messages: **(1) Control**, no override (today's behavior), and **(2) Customized**, `username: "Pulse"` + `icon_emoji: "📟"` (raw Unicode, not `:shortcode:`). `chat.postMessage` responses (post-call echo): ```jsonc // (1) CONTROL — no override { "ok": true, "ts": "…820.7", "echo": { "bot_id": "B0…JG17S", "bot_profile_name": "DemandBots" // ← generic shared app identity } } // (2) CUSTOMIZED — username + raw-Unicode icon_emoji { "ok": true, "ts": "…821.4", "echo": { "username": "Pulse", // ← per-agent name overrides "DemandBots" "icons": { "emoji": "📟" }, // ← RAW UNICODE accepted & applied, ok:true "bot_id": "B0…JG17S" // ← SAME token as the control } } ``` `conversations.history` read-back (the authoritative persisted render): ```jsonc [ { "ts": "…821.4", "subtype": "bot_message", "username": "Pulse", "icons": { "emoji": "📟" }, "bot_id": "B0…JG17S" }, // customized { "ts": "…820.7", "bot_profile": { "name": "DemandBots" }, "bot_id": "B0…JG17S" } // control ] ``` And the same two messages as a client renders them: The control lands under the generic **"DemandBots"** app; the customized one lands under **"Pulse 📟"**. (This is the render of the controlled `chat.postMessage` diagnostic — the Slack-side behavior for the exact call the adapter emits — not an OC-generated heartbeat post.) So Part 2 nails down two things: 1. **Slack accepts raw-Unicode `icon_emoji`.** `📟` went through verbatim → `ok:true`, and it's persisted as `icons.emoji:"📟"` in both the echo and `conversations.history`. No error, no `:emoji_not_found:`, no fallback. That directly clears the P2 / `merge-risk: message-delivery` worry that Slack might reject raw Unicode here — it doesn't, and the passthrough needs no normalization. 2. **The `username` overlay renders the per-agent persona**, distinct from the app default, on a `chat:write.customize` workspace — same token, "DemandBots" vs "Pulse", seconds apart. What Part 2 doesn't re-prove (because Part 1 already does): that OC threads the configured identity into that `chat.postMessage`. That's the job of the new tests and the patched `buildContext()` (now forwards `identity: opts.identity`). ### Before (current `main` / 2026.5.26, unpatched) - 8:07 AM — a heartbeat/cron **announce** landing as the generic **"DemandBots"** app. - 4:48 PM — an operator messaging the agent's channel. - 4:48 PM — the same agent's **@-mention reply** rendering correctly aliased (the post-#38235 reply path). That contrast is the whole point: reply works, announce/heartbeat doesn't. The #84297 write-up (evidence A/B/C) documents the broken behavior on v2026.5.18 — scope granted, `buildContext()` dropping `opts.identity`, and the delivery mirror showing zero `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` references. ### The one thing left for a maintainer A fully OC-generated heartbeat fire (`runHeartbeatOnce` / scheduler) into a live `chat:write.customize` workspace, to eyeball the bubble end-to-end. The reporter's diagnostic proves Slack accepts and renders the call; the new tests prove OC produces that call; the only unrun step is gluing those together live. A maintainer can do it with `@openclaw-mantis slack desktop smoke: verify an agent heartbeat to Slack with identity name and emoji renders under that persona.` ## Root Cause Three layered gaps: 1. **The heartbeat runner never forwarded identity.** Both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls (heartbeat-ok around L1701, main reply around L1981) passed `session: outboundSession` and `deps: opts.deps`, but no `identity`. 2. **The `runtimeSend` factory dropped it too.** `buildContext()` in `channel-outbound-send.ts` didn't carry `opts.identity` through on the legacy CLI path. 3. **The Slack adapter filtered out raw Unicode.** Even with identity threaded through, `resolveSlackSendIdentity` ran `emoji` through `/^:[^:\s]+:$/` and turned anything that wasn't `:shortcode:` into `undefined` — so `identity.emoji: "📟"` got stripped before it ever reached `icon_emoji`. Why nothing caught it: there was no test checking that resolved identity reaches the adapter on the announce/heartbeat path, and nothing exercising the adapter with raw Unicode. #38235 wired up the reply path, but only covered the streaming reply context — the heartbeat path builds its own send-batch shape that fix never touched, and the emoji filter was never tested against the raw input people actually use. Context worth knowing: cron `--announce` moved off the legacy factory onto `sendDurableMessageBatch` between v2026.5.18 and `main`, and happened to thread identity correctly on the way. Heartbeat went through the same migration but kept the bug. That's why the reporter's v2026.5.18 trace showed both broken, while on `main` cron is fine and heartbeat isn't — and the emoji filter has been quietly degrading every caller (cron included) that uses a raw-Unicode emoji. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - **Where:** `src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts` (4 new cases), `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.identity.test.ts` (new file, 2 cases driving `runHeartbeatOnce` against a real Slack test plugin), and `extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts` (4 cases: raw Unicode, `:shortcode:`, `iconUrl`-wins, no-identity). - **What they lock in:** when `agents.list[].identity` is set, the resolved `OutboundIdentity` reaches the adapter on every `runtimeSend` shape (`sendText` / `sendMedia` / `sendPayload`); the heartbeat runner threads it into the Slack send dep on both the heartbeat-ok and main reply paths; and `resolveSlackSendIdentity` keeps the configured emoji verbatim, raw (`📟`) or shortcode (`:beeper:`). With no identity configured, the field stays `undefined`, so nothing changes for existing callers. - **Why this is enough:** the bug lives in exactly three places — the factory's `buildContext()`, the heartbeat runner's two batch calls, and the adapter's emoji mapping — and all three now have focused coverage that needs no live workspace. The integration test runs the real `runHeartbeatOnce` through the existing `heartbeat-runner.test-harness` Slack plugin, so a regression at any layer fails a test. - **Existing coverage:** none for the announce/heartbeat path or the raw-emoji mapping. `send.identity-fallback.test.ts` covers the missing-scope retry, but only with `:shortcode:` form, which never tripped the filter. - If no new test is added: N/A — new tests are added. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Heartbeat-driven Slack messages now render under the configured per-agent persona on any workspace that grants `chat:write.customize` — matching what the reply path has done since #38235. `agents.list[].identity.emoji` can now be a raw Unicode emoji (`"📟"`) and it's applied verbatim to `chat.postMessage.icon_emoji`; previously this adapter path only honored `:shortcode:`. Workspaces without `chat:write.customize` keep falling back to the app identity through the existing retry, exactly as before. Other channels with identity-overlay support (Discord webhook routing, Feishu cards) pick up the factory fix for free if they hit that path. No new config and no new flags — this just honors the `agents.list[].identity` you already have, on paths that used to drop or filter it. ## Diagram How the heartbeat path and the adapter behave before vs. after: ```text Before (heartbeat path): [heartbeat tick] -> resolveDeliveryTarget -> outboundSession -> sendDurableMessageBatch({ session, deps }) ↑ identity dropped here -> Slack adapter sees ctx.identity = undefined -> chat.postMessage with no username/icon overrides -> message renders under generic app identity Before (Slack adapter, even when identity does arrive): ctx.identity.emoji = "📟" -> resolveSlackSendIdentity() -> /^:[^:\s]+:$/.test("📟") === false -> iconEmoji = undefined ← raw Unicode silently dropped -> chat.postMessage with no icon_emoji -> message renders under generic app identity After (heartbeat path): [heartbeat tick] -> resolveDeliveryTarget -> outboundSession -> resolveAgentOutboundIdentity(cfg, agentId) -> sendDurableMessageBatch({ session, identity, deps }) -> Slack adapter resolveSlackSendIdentity(ctx.identity) -> chat.postMessage with username + icon_emoji -> message renders under "Pulse 📟" After (Slack adapter): ctx.identity.emoji = "📟" (or ":beeper:") -> resolveSlackSendIdentity() -> iconEmoji = ctx.identity.emoji ← raw value passed through -> chat.postMessage with icon_emoji = "📟" -> message renders under the configured persona Legacy runtimeSend factory (channel-outbound-send.ts): Before: buildContext() -> { cfg, to, text, ... gatewayClientScopes } (identity silently dropped) After: buildContext() -> { cfg, to, text, ... gatewayClientScopes, identity } ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No — it's the same `chat.postMessage` call, just with `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` filled in from existing config when the agent has an identity overlay. - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - Channel: Slack via `@openclaw/slack`, socket mode. The bot scope needs `chat:write.customize` for the overlay to take effect server-side. Heartbeat set to `target: "slack"`, `to: "channel:C0XXXXXXX"`. - Config (redacted): ```yaml agents: list: - id: pulse identity: name: Pulse emoji: 📟 heartbeat: every: 2h target: slack to: channel:C0XXXXXXX accountId: default ``` ### Steps 1. Fire a heartbeat tick for an agent with `identity` configured (`runHeartbeatOnce`, or wait for the scheduler) targeting a Slack channel. 2. Watch the message land in the channel. 3. Before this patch it shows up under the generic app bot + default icon. After, it shows up under "Pulse 📟" on any `chat:write.customize` workspace — whether the emoji is raw Unicode or `:shortcode:`. ### Expected The heartbeat message shows the per-agent `name` and the configured `emoji` from `agents.list[].identity`, just like the reply path does post-#38235. ### Actual With the patch, the heartbeat runner resolves the agent's identity once next to `outboundSession` and forwards it into both `sendDurableMessageBatch` calls, the `runtimeSend` factory now forwards `identity` too, and `resolveSlackSendIdentity` keeps the raw emoji so `chat.postMessage.icon_emoji` carries it through. The reporter's live diagnostic (**Real behavior proof → Part 2**) confirms Slack accepts the raw `icon_emoji` and renders the per-agent `username` for that exact call. ## Human Verification (required) Verified: - `RuntimeSendOpts.identity` reaches `ChannelOutboundContext.identity` for `sendText`, `sendMedia`, and `sendPayload` (block) sends. - `runtimeSend.sendMessage(...)` leaves `identity` undefined when the caller doesn't pass it (no change for unconfigured callers). - `runHeartbeatOnce` resolves the agent's identity from `agents.list[].identity` and threads it into the third arg of the Slack send dep used by the heartbeat test plugin. - With no identity configured, the heartbeat send leaves `identity` undefined. - `resolveSlackSendIdentity` keeps raw Unicode emoji (`📟`) on `iconEmoji`. - `resolveSlackSendIdentity` still accepts `:shortcode:` form (`":beeper:"`). - `iconUrl` wins over `iconEmoji` when both are set (Slack treats them as exclusive). - Cron `--announce` already passes identity on `main` (`src/cron/delivery.ts`, lines 68 / 105) — confirmed by reading the path; no new wiring needed. - **Live, by @aw-stevens on the real `chat:write.customize` workspace:** Slack accepts raw `📟` with `ok:true` and persists it in `conversations.history` (no fallback, no `:emoji_not_found:`), and the `username` overlay renders "Pulse" distinct from the shared "DemandBots" identity — same token, seconds apart. Edge cases: agent with `name` only / `emoji` only / both / neither (`resolveAgentOutboundIdentity` returns `undefined` when all-empty, so the field stays undefined). The heartbeat-ok and main reply sends carry the same resolved identity. Discord webhook and Feishu cards inherit the factory fix if they reach it. Workspaces without `chat:write.customize` hit the existing missing-scope retry — unchanged. What I couldn't do myself: a live `chat:write.customize` run from this machine (no spare bot/workspace). The reporter's diagnostic covers the Slack acceptance + render half; the only step nobody's run is a fully OC-generated heartbeat into a live workspace (vs. the controlled `chat.postMessage` of the same shape). Their `auth.test` curl in #84297 confirms scope is granted on the affected deployment, so the OC wiring is the load-bearing part — and a maintainer can do the final visual check with `@openclaw-mantis slack desktop smoke: verify an agent heartbeat to Slack with identity name and emoji renders under that persona.` ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes. `RuntimeSendOpts.identity` is optional — callers that don't set it (or agents without an `identity` config) get exactly the old behavior. Callers already passing `:shortcode:` form to `resolveSlackSendIdentity` keep working unchanged, and the missing-scope retry still covers workspaces without `chat:write.customize`. - Config/env changes? None. `agents.list[].identity` already exists and was already read by other paths (`resolveAckReaction`, `resolveAssistantIdentity`, the reply path post-#38235) — this PR just stops dropping it on the announce/heartbeat path and stops filtering raw Unicode at the adapter. - Migration needed? No. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. ## Risks and Mitigations - **A workspace without `chat:write.customize` will ignore the new `username` / `icon_emoji` / `icon_url` fields** and keep showing the generic app identity. - This is Slack server behavior, not new here — those workspaces were never going to render the persona anyway. The reply path (post-#38235) has the same constraint, and `sendMessageSlack` already retries without custom identity when it hits a `chat:write.customize` scope error. - **A misconfigured `identity.emoji` with arbitrary text (e.g. `"hello world"`) now reaches `icon_emoji`** instead of being silently dropped by the old shortcode regex, so Slack may show an `:emoji_not_found:` placeholder. - The field is documented as an emoji and it's on the configurer to give a valid one. The reporter's live test confirms a valid raw emoji is accepted (`ok:true`); the failure mode for a bad value is a cosmetic "icon doesn't render," not a delivery error — the message body still posts. Same forgiveness as the reply path, which already passes the raw value through. - **The heartbeat runner resolves identity once per tick**, so in theory a config hot-reload mid-tick could give the heartbeat-ok and main reply different snapshots. - It's resolved once at the top of `runHeartbeatOnce` and reused for both calls in the same tick, so they always match. The next tick picks up any reloaded config naturally. - **A downstream caller of `runtimeSend.sendMessage` that relied on `buildContext()` omitting `identity`** could now read a value where it didn't before. - The field is only populated when the caller passes `opts.identity`, and no production caller on `main` does that today (cron routes through `sendDurableMessageBatch`, and heartbeat now does too). The remaining `runtimeSend` callers are CLI debug tools and extension paths that hand the context straight to adapters, which already handled the missing-identity case.
target.author: rohang2005

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
